steps.title: Understand the effect of adding a constant to all values
steps.content:
When a constant is added to every value in a dataset, two key things happen:
  • The median shifts by that same constant. If you add 56 to all values, the median also increases by 56.
  • The range stays the same. Range = max − min. When both the maximum and minimum shift by 56, their difference remains unchanged.

steps.title: Apply the rule to data set B
steps.content:
Data set B is formed by adding 56 to each value in data set A.
  • Median of B = Median of A + 56, so median of B is greater than median of A.
  • Range of B = (maxB − minB) = (maxA + 56) − (minA + 56) = maxA − minA = Range of A, so the ranges are equal.
This matches choice C — C is correct.
